A Validated Numerical Laboratory for Chaotic Pendulum Dynamics

Elliot Jung — generated 2026-06-10 — Pendulum Lab v10.25 — all numbers in this report are computed by the same code path the application, its Web Worker, the CLI and the unit suite share.
Abstract. A TypeScript laboratory for nonlinear pendulum dynamics with twelve measured-order integrators, exact analytic Jacobians, and a chaos-diagnostics suite whose every claim is gated by an independent check: the Lyapunov spectrum is verified against Hamiltonian constraints, chaos verdicts are cross-validated by the Jacobian-free 0–1 test and recurrence quantification, the flip-basin boundary is quantified by basin entropy, box-counting dimension and a Wada-property candidate test, and the whole engine is cross-validated against an independently derived SciPy DOP853 reference and a 31-digit double-double ground truth. The period-doubling route to chaos of the damped driven pendulum is traced with Newton/Floquet continuation including branch switching onto the period-2 orbit.

1. System and headline diagnostics

All chaos diagnostics below use the equal-mass double pendulum (m₁ = m₂ = 1, l₁ = 1.2, l₂ = 1, g = 9.81) from the chaotic initial state (θ₁, θ₂, ω₁, ω₂) = (2, 2.5, 0, 0), the application's default preset.

Maximal Lyapunov λ₁ 1.1340 ± 0.1056 (batched-means SE)
Spectrum sum Σλ -4.59e-4 (Hamiltonian constraint: ≈ 0)
Kaplan–Yorke dimension 4.000
Consistency gate PASS (|Σλ| ≤ 0.1, pairing error 0.022, 1 zero exponents)
0–1 test K 0.998 (≈ 1 ⇒ chaotic; Jacobian-free, independent of the spectrum)
RQA determinism / divergence 0.505 / 0.0417
FTLE field range (T = 5) [0.26, 2.54]

ExponentValueBlock SE
λ11.1340± 0.1056
λ20.0656± 0.0280
λ3-0.0439± 0.0350
λ4-1.1562± 0.0951

2. Fractal exit structure and Wada candidacy

Colouring each initial angle pair by which rod flips first yields the classic double-pendulum fractal. Quantitatively (n = 120 grid):

Basin entropy Sb 0.167
Boundary basin entropy Sbb 0.458 (Sbb > ln 2 ≈ 0.693 is a sufficient fractality condition)
Box-counting dimension 1.414 (strictly between curve and plane)
Wada fraction 0.5% of boundary cells touch ≥ 3 basins (below the candidacy threshold at this resolution; the fraction grows under refinement)

3. The period-doubling route to chaos (driven pendulum)

For the damped driven pendulum (γ = 0.5, ωD = 2/3) the oscillating period-1 orbit is continued in drive amplitude A with Newton on the stroboscopic map; its real Floquet multiplier crosses −1 between A = 1.065 (μ = -0.951) and A = 1.07 (μ = -1.130) — the textbook APD ≈ 1.066. Switching maps to P² along the critical eigenvector lands on the period-2 orbit:

Branch switch at A = 1.07 converged (residual 2.55e-11)
Period-2 orbit (θ, ω) = (-0.3202, 1.9912), separation 0.117 from the period-1 point
Stability stable (the attractor just past onset — matches direct simulation); chaos follows near A ≈ 1.08

4. Numerical credibility

Measured convergence orders (Richardson self-convergence on this exact system; no analytic reference assumed):

IntegratorMeasured order
euler0.81
rk22.18
rk44.02
gauss24.00

External cross-validation against an independently derived SciPy reference (different language, different derivation of the equations of motion, different integrator family — solve_ivp DOP853 at rtol = atol = 1e-13):

CaseHorizonMax ‖Δ‖∞BoundVerdict
regular small-angle20 s4.12e-141.00e-8PASS
chaotic10 s6.40e-111.00e-5PASS

For the chaotic case the divergence is the shared tolerance floor amplified by eλ₁t — the expected signature of two correct implementations of the same chaotic flow. A 31-digit double-double reference additionally measures the float64 predictability horizon itself (~1e-14 at t = 2 s growing to decorrelation by t ≈ 20 s; see documents/known-limitations.md).

5. Figures

6. Reproducibility

Every figure above is a capture of the running application (no offline re-plotting); every number is produced by the same runChaosJob handler the in-app worker executes, callable headlessly via npm run research. The full pipeline — 274 unit tests, 26 browser end-to-end tests, integrator reference validation, and the SciPy cross-check — runs in CI. Regenerate this document with npm run notebook.
